evals.name: Watch Loop Clarity
evals.question: Does the skill clearly instruct agents to use agentation_watch_annotations as the primary interaction pattern?
evals.pass_criterion: Watch loop is prominent and explained before other patterns
evals.fail_criterion: Watch loop is buried or unclear

evals.name: Acknowledge-Resolve Flow
evals.question: Does the skill clearly instruct acknowledge → fix → resolve lifecycle for each annotation?
evals.pass_criterion: Lifecycle is explicit with correct tool names
evals.fail_criterion: Lifecycle is missing, incomplete, or uses wrong tool names

evals.name: elementPath as Grep Target
evals.question: Does the skill clearly instruct agents to use elementPath CSS selector as the primary code search target?
evals.fail_criterion: elementPath usage is not mentioned or buried

evals.name: Platform Detection
evals.question: Does the skill clearly distinguish between MCP mode (Claude/Codex/Gemini/OpenCode) and Copy-Paste mode?
evals.pass_criterion: Both modes are clear with when to use each

evals.name: omg Phase Guard
evals.question: Does the skill clearly state agentation only operates in verify/verify_ui phase, never in plan phase?

test_inputs.description: annotate fix the login button color
watch mode — review the dashboard layout
Copy the annotation and paste here

versions.description: Original SKILL.md — mixes setup docs with agent instructions

notes.EVAL1: Watch loop buried at line 475 (Section 6 Pattern 2), after 440 lines of setup/install docs
notes.EVAL2: Pattern 2 shows full acknowledge→resolve lifecycle with correct tool names — passes for MCP inputs, T4 has no server so N/A scored 0
notes.EVAL3: elementPath mentioned in Pattern 2 and Best Practice #6 but not prominently surfaced at the top
notes.EVAL4: Copy-Paste vs Agent Sync table exists in Section 2 Architecture but drowns in setup content
notes.EVAL5: Phase guard table exists in Section 12 (line 716-718) but completely invisible without reading entire document

versions.description: Rewritten SKILL.md — agent-focused, Key Rules at top, setup extracted to references/

notes.EVAL1: Watch loop is the first substantive section after Key Rules — agents see it immediately
notes.EVAL2: Acknowledge-Resolve flow explicit in Primary Pattern with correct tool names + quick reference table
notes.EVAL3: elementPath → grep connection explicit in Key Rules (#3) and Watch Loop section
notes.EVAL4: Mode Detection section up front distinguishes MCP vs Copy-Paste with clear when-to-use
notes.EVAL5: Phase Guard in Key Rules (#5) — first thing agents read
notes.T4_note: Copy-paste mode has no server so EVAL2 (ack/resolve) and EVAL3 (elementPath grep) are N/A — scored 0 as not applicable without MCP

key_changes: Added Key Rules section at top (5 rules mirroring NEVER pattern from omg)
Moved Watch Loop to primary pattern — first content after Key Rules
Made elementPath → grep connection explicit in Key Rules and Watch Loop
Added Mode Detection section before any workflow patterns
Phase guard now in Key Rules — visible without reading full document
Extracted 440+ lines of setup to references/setup-guide.md
Collapsed platform config blocks into compact support table
Kept Annotation schema but made it more compact (lifecycle diagram inline)
